creator: rea-fbsd@codelabs.ru
text:
Jo, good day.

Wed, Jan 09, 2008 at 10:25:32AM -0800, Jo Rhett wrote:
> On Jan 8, 2008, at 3:50 AM, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
>> May I first note that the port maintainer said 'USE_BDB?=   44',
>> so this can be overriden by the administrator.  I decided to use
>> 4.3 at the time of the PR submission, because the system used BDB
>> 4.3 everywhere and cfengine works with that version just fine.
>> There is no strict technical reasons why not to use 4.4, but I found
>> no reasons not to use the older BDB packages.
> 
> You say works, then say patches are necessary?  Not trying to give you a 
> hard time, but if patches are necessary then it doesn't work.

OK, I should be more clear: I wanted to use 4.3, because I had used
4.3 on Linux and it worked.  I had tried to compile FreeBSD port
with it and noticed that I had dynamically linked to 4.0.  Then I
created the patch to the configure and linked against 4.3.  It
worked.

> 
>> The CFEngine manual at 
>> http://www.cfengine.org/docs/cfengine-Reference.html
> 
> is very often out of date ;-)  Best to submit patches for that.

May be.  But why are you telling that BDB >= 4.4 is needed?
Do you have some negative experience?

>> So BDB >= 3.2 will go, if the manual is not very outdated.  Moreover,
>> Mark Burgess accepted those patches, so I believe that they are correct.
> 
> Ah, that's the crucial part.  If true, then it's patched at the source.

Yes, it was patched.  And this was mentioned in the follow-up to the PR.

>> Yes, the changes were accepted to the mainstream CFEngine.  But at
>> the time of submission there were no releases with that patches,
>> so the best I can do is to submit the patches for the FreeBSD port
>> to make it work 'just now' for other people, not just for me.
> 
> Right now we have a problem in that far too many things that should be 
> fixed in the mainline code are being patched locally.  I'd rather get all 
> of those fixes into the main distribution (I can help) and minimize our 
> local patches if possible.

I am submitting patches both to the current port and to the maintainer
of the mainline code.  And I am trying to push both of them, because
the port for the next version can be delayed (or the next version
can be delayed by itself) and I prefer the ports to make their job.

And if you said that you can help to get the fixes to the CFEngine
mainline code, then I will try to exploit this, if you're not against
it.  I had sent another patch to Mark some months ago and he promised
to analyze it once he will have time.  I will check for the state of
that patch and if it still not brought to the mainline code, I will
sent it to you, if you will be so kind to review it and push to the
sources.

Will drop you a mail by the end of week.
-- 
Eygene
